layout_dets.html:
Algorithm 1 Beam Aggregation Reasoning
Require: Complex multi-hop questions, Q Require: Multi-source knowledge retriever, R Require: Large language model, LLM
Require: Candidate size in aggregation, k 1: Qdecomp = LLM(Q)
2: for N (i) in PostOrderTraverse(Qdecomp) do
3: if N (i) is leaf-node then
a = LLM (q(i), R) 4:
a(i), p(i) = Vote(âa)[1 : k] 5:
6: else
7: initialize â, p = list, list
8: for c′, p′ in CartProd(sons(N (i)) do
9: q′ = MaskFill(qi, c′)
10: at, pt = Vote(LLM(q′, c′, R))
11: â ← Concat(à, at) p ← Concat(p, pt · p′)
12:
13: end for
a(i), p(i) = Aggr(a, p)[1 : k] 14:
15: end if 16: end for

Dim / Contains
SymbolDescription
(a) InputsComplex knowledge-intensive multi-hop question.
Q R(...)Multi-source knowledge retriever.
LLM (...)Large language model prompted with few-shot demon-
stration. LLM (. . . , R) indicates that the reasoning is augmented by the retriever.
kHyper-parameter: Candidate size in beam aggregation.
(b) Question Tree Qdecomp{N (1), N (2) . . . }Decomposed questions tree, contains tree nodes. The
root node Nroot represents the original question, and the children of a node sons(N (i) are sub-questions. Tree node i, contains the sub-question, answers and
N (i) q(i){q(i), a(i), p(i)} Rprobabilities. Sub-question for node i, obtained in decompose step. The question may contain placeholder tokens (e.g.
#1) which need to be replaced by the answer in sub- questions (sons) during reasoning.
a(i), p(i)Rk, RkTop-k candidate answers and corresponding probabili- ties in node i, obtained in bottom-up reasoning step.
(c) Variables in Reasoning Step $aR?, R?Intermediate results for answers and corresponding prob-
abilities, need to be further voted or aggregated. Same as above.
at , pt c',p′R', R? R/sons|, ROne combination of sub-questions answer and cor- responding probabilities. A combination is one ele-
ment in the cartesian product of the children's answers c′ × a(i) = asons1 × asons2 . . . and p′ is the pro- isons duction of children's probabilities for this combination.
(d) Functions in Pseudocode PostOrderTraverse(Q)[N] → [N]Return a new node sequence in post-order (child0 <
CartProd(sons)[N] → [[a], p]child1 < .. < root). Cartesian product of the answers and the associated
probabilities of the children. For instance, if a node has two children x, y, this function will return a set (x) (y) (x)p(y) |i, j = 1, 2, . . . , k}. >, p()p(g
MaskFill(q, c)q, [a] → qReplaces the placeholder token (#i) in the sub-question q with candidate answer ci.
Vote(a)[a] → [a], [p]Deduplicate answers and obtain a probabilities distribu- tion based on their frequency. Returns are arranged in
Aggr(a, p)[a], [p] → [a], [p]descending order of probabilities. Return the unique answers and their normalized proba- bilities, arranged in descending order of probabilities.
